text: I think what Manifest explores is basic concepts of morality that all civilizations and societies have explored, as well as policies, laws, codes and ethics that are all universal. We are judged and held accountable and we always have been down through the ages. Furthermore, it addresses that science has its place, but is in its infancy in terms of the thoughts, beliefs and practices that came long before it. The divine has always been a consideration since the first hominid raised his head to contemplate how and why. Maybe Manifest is but a modern parable intended to challenge who and why we are in the face of something inexplicable and apparently polarizing (as you will see.)
label: theory
notes: Thematic meta-commentary about what the show is about rather than a structured plot hypothesis - boundary with rant_rave

text: I don't think that Manifest is demanding a particular point of view, simply exploring it. Clearly something is going on that cannot be explained with the usual tangible proofs. For me, I rather like the religious, or perhaps more accurately, the spiritual side of things. How did our ancestors approach the unknown or social responsibility, morality and justice. Those kinds of things. And, honestly, there are so many sci-fi, alien invasion, rogue goverment operation type films and novels...exploring spiritual causes and effects is rather refreshing, for me.
label: theory
notes: Meta-commentary/opinion about the shows approach - boundary with rant_rave
